{{small|Warrington Wolves, 4 June}} | low_attendance = 1,748
{{small|York Acorn, 12 March}} | top_tries = [[Ben Cockayne]] (17) | top_goals = [[Gareth Morton]] (59) | top_points = Gareth Morton (146){{cite web |title=National League One 2006 - Hull Kingston Rovers |url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/national-league-one-2006/hull-kr/summary.html |publisher=Rugby League Project |access-date=20 December 2025}} | prev_season = [[2005 Hull Kingston Rovers season|2005]] | next_season = [[2007 Hull Kingston Rovers season|2007]] | list = yes | list_link = List of Hull Kingston Rovers seasons }} The 2006 season was '''[[Hull Kingston Rovers]]'''{{'}} ninth consecutive season playing in England's second tier of [[rugby league]]. During the season, they competed in the [[2006 National League Cup]], the [[2006 Rugby League National Leagues#National League One|2006 National League One]] season, and the [[2006 Challenge Cup]]. 2006 was Hull Kingston Rovers' first season in which they fielded a squad of fully professional players, funded by sponsorships from companies such as [[Lloyds TSB]].{{cite news |last1=Ledger |first1=John |title=Robins set to fly high again |date=24 December 2005|newspaper=The Yorkshire Post|location=Leeds|page=1|id={{ProQuest|335292346}} |quote=That extra [sponsorship] income will enable Rovers to become the only existing National League One club to have a full-time squad next season, when the level of competition will be even greater than it was this summer following the relegation from Super League of Leigh and Widnes.}}{{cite web |title=Big bank's backing the Robins! |url=http://www.hullkr.co.uk/indexafter.php |publisher=Hull Kingston Rovers |access-date=31 January 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20051202024739/http://www.hullkr.co.uk/indexafter.php |archive-date=2 December 2005 |date=30 November 2005}} The club's victory in the National League One final against [[Widnes Vikings]] resulted in their promotion to the [[Super League]], marking Hull Kingston Rovers' return to top-flight rugby league for the first time in 20 years. ==Preseason
